report_name: WordPress Vendor 'ShapedPlugin' Hit by Supply Chain Attack Delivering Credential-Stealing Malware

attack_patterns.name: Credentials from Web Browsers
attack_patterns.description: The fake WooCommerce plugin contained functions to intercept and steal credentials by hooking into the WordPress login process.

attack_patterns.name: Server Software Component: Web Shell
attack_patterns.description: The malicious fake WooCommerce plugin provided backdoor access, allowing attackers to modify site content or inject further malicious code.

attack_patterns.name: Compromise Software Supply Chain
attack_patterns.description: Attackers compromised the infrastructure of the WordPress plugin vendor ShapedPlugin to inject malicious code into the updates for three legitimate, paid plugins.

malware.name: Fake WooCommerce Plugin
malware.description: A counterfeit WooCommerce plugin, installed via malicious updates from ShapedPlugin, that acts as a backdoor and credential stealer. It is designed to steal administrator passwords, database credentials, and 2FA codes, and allow attackers to modify website content.

identities.name: ShapedPlugin
identities.description: A vendor of premium WordPress plugins whose update mechanism was compromised in a supply chain attack to distribute malicious updates containing a backdoor.

infrastructure.name: ShapedPlugin Update Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: The official update mechanism for ShapedPlugin's paid plugins, which was compromised by attackers to distribute malicious updates.

indicators.name: WordPress wp-config.php Modification
indicators.pattern:
title: WordPress wp-config.php Modification
id: 4f5e6d7c-1a2b-4c3d-8e9f-0a1b2c3d4e5f
status: experimental
description: Detects modification of the WordPress wp-config.php file, which can be a sign of compromise, such as credential theft or malware injection.
references:
  - https://research.checkpoint.com/2026/22nd-june-threat-intelligence-report/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-22
tags:
  - attack.t1565.001
level: high
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: linux
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith: '/wp-config.php'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate administrative changes to the WordPress configuration.
indicators.description: Detects modifications to the wp-config.php file, a potential sign of compromise as indicated by the hunting hints.

indicators.name: WordPress New Administrator Account Creation
indicators.pattern:
title: WordPress New Administrator Account Creation
id: 9a8b7c6d-5e4f-4d3c-b2a1-f0e9d8c7b6a5
status: experimental
description: Detects the creation of a new administrator-level user account in WordPress, which could indicate an attacker creating a persistent backdoor account.
references:
  - https://research.checkpoint.com/2026/22nd-june-threat-intelligence-report/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-22
tags:
  - attack.t1136.001
level: medium
logsource:
  category: webserver
detection:
  selection:
    c-uri-query|contains|all:
      - 'action=createuser'
      - 'role=administrator'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate creation of new administrator accounts.
indicators.description: Detects the creation of new administrator accounts in WordPress, a common post-compromise action mentioned in the hunting hints.
